daily_indaba
  • daily_indaba
  • daily_indaba package
  • daily_indaba.views package
  • View page source

daily_indaba.views package

Submodules

daily_indaba.views.articles module

Article views for The Daily Indaba.

Public views: - home: Home page showing the 8 most recent approved articles with

teasers; accessible to all visitors.

Authenticated article views: - article_list: Approved article listing with optional importance

filter; editors also see pending articles in their scope.

  • article_detail: Single article with threaded comments; full content gated by subscription or staff role.

Journalist/editor management views: - article_create: Journalist creates a new article (pending approval). - article_edit: Journalist edits their own unapproved article, or

editor edits any article in their scope.

  • article_delete: Journalist deletes their own unapproved article, or editor deletes any article in their scope; confirmation page on GET.

Editorial workflow views (POST only): - article_approve: Editor approves a pending article; triggers

subscriber notification emails.

  • article_reject: Editor returns a pending article for revision; optionally emails the journalist a rejection reason.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_approve(request, pk)[source]

Approve an article (editor only).

Sets approved=True and publication_date. Subscriber emails are dispatched by the article’s post-save signal after the approval is saved.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_create(request)[source]

Allow a journalist to create a new article.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_delete(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own unapproved article only) or editor to delete an article. Renders a confirmation page on GET; deletes on POST.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_detail(request, pk)[source]

Render a single article with its threaded comment section.

Readers without a qualifying subscription see only the title, teaser (first sentence), and a subscribe prompt. Full access requires an active Subscription to the article’s author or publisher, or the all-articles flat-rate plan. Journalists and editors always have full access.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_edit(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist to edit their own unapproved article, or an editor to edit any article.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_list(request)[source]

Render the authenticated article listing page.

Supports filtering by importance level (GET param importance). The left sidebar displays importance-level links mirroring the eCommerce category panel.

daily_indaba.views.articles.article_reject(request, pk)[source]

Reject (return) an article submission (editor only).

The article leaves the approval queue and is marked as returned so the journalist can revise and resubmit it. If a reason is supplied it is emailed to the journalist and stored on the article for in-app display.

daily_indaba.views.articles.home(request)[source]

Render the public home page.

Accessible to all visitors (no login required). Displays a one-sentence site description and the eight most recent approved articles, each shown with its title and the first sentence of its content only.

daily_indaba.views.comments module

Comment views for The Daily Indaba.

Reader-only; all views require login and the ‘reader’ role.

  • post_comment: Post a new comment or threaded reply on an approved article; requires an active subscription or all-articles plan.

  • comment_confirm_delete: Render a confirmation page before deleting the reader’s own comment thread.

  • comment_delete: Delete the reader’s own comment thread (POST only).

daily_indaba.views.comments.comment_confirm_delete(request, article_pk, comment_pk)[source]

Render a confirmation page before deleting a reader comment thread.

daily_indaba.views.comments.comment_delete(request, article_pk, comment_pk)[source]

Allow a reader to delete their own comment thread.

daily_indaba.views.comments.post_comment(request, pk)[source]

Create a comment or reply on an article (readers only).

On validation failure the user is redirected back to the article with an error message rather than re-rendering the form, to keep the detail view simple.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards module

Dashboard and subscription views for The Daily Indaba.

Role dashboards: - journalist_dashboard: Journalist’s personal desk showing their

articles, newsletters, and subscription fee form.

  • editor_dashboard: Editor overview with pending approval queue summary and recent approvals.

  • approval_queue: Full pending article queue for editors, with inline rejection form.

Subscription views (POST only, reader role): - toggle_subscription: Subscribe or unsubscribe a reader to/from a

publisher or journalist; sends confirmation email on new sub.

  • subscribe_all_articles: Toggle the reader’s flat-rate all-articles plan; sends confirmation email on activation.

Fee management views (POST only): - set_journalist_fee: Journalist updates their monthly reader

subscription fee within the policy-defined range.

  • publisher_set_fee: Publisher owner or affiliated editor updates the publisher’s monthly subscription fee.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.approval_queue(request)[source]

Render the full article approval queue (editor only).

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.editor_dashboard(request)[source]

Render the editor overview dashboard with approval queue summary.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Render a journalist’s personal desk (articles + newsletters).

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.publisher_set_fee(request, pk)[source]

Allow a publisher owner or affiliated editor to set the fee.

Valid range comes from the seeded pricing policy. Redirects back to the publisher’s profile page.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.set_journalist_fee(request)[source]

Allow a journalist to set their configured monthly subscription fee.

Validates the submitted fee and updates journalist_monthly_fee on the user. Redirects back to the journalist dashboard.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.subscribe_all_articles(request)[source]

Toggle the reader’s configured all-articles flat-rate plan.

Flips all_articles_plan and saves. When activated, a confirmation email is sent to the reader. Redirects to the subscriptions management page. Only readers may access this view.

daily_indaba.views.dashboards.toggle_subscription(request)[source]

Toggle a reader’s subscription to a publisher or journalist.

Expects POST fields type (publisher | journalist) and id (primary key of the target). On a new subscription a confirmation email is sent to the reader. Redirects back to the referring page (or home if no referrer is set).

daily_indaba.views.helpers module

Shared helpers, constants, and utilities used across view modules.

Constants: - SITE_DESCRIPTION: One-line platform tagline used on the home page. - IMPORTANCE_LABELS: Human-readable labels for article importance levels.

Role-gate helpers: - _require_role: Redirect unauthorized or unauthenticated users; returns

False when access is allowed.

  • _user_has_full_access: Return True when an authenticated user may read the full article body (staff role, all-articles plan, or active sub).

Email helpers: - _notify_subscribers: Email all relevant subscribers when an article

is approved; delivery failures are logged but never propagated.

  • _send_subscription_confirmation: Email a reader when they create a new publisher or journalist subscription.

Text helpers: - _first_sentence: Extract the first sentence of article content as

plain text (max 250 chars) for use as a card teaser.

Form/queryset helpers: - _limit_publisher_choices: Restrict the publisher dropdown to the

journalist’s affiliated publishers.

  • _editor_can_curate_independent_articles: Return True when the editor is affiliated with an independent-desk publisher.

  • _filter_articles_for_editor: Scope an article queryset to the editor’s assigned publisher range.

  • _editor_can_manage_article: Return True when the editor may curate the given article.

  • _publisher_account_owns_publisher: Return True when the signed-in publisher account owns the given publisher.

  • _user_can_manage_publisher_settings: Return True when the user may manage a publisher’s fee and settings.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters module

Newsletter views for The Daily Indaba.

  • newsletter_list: All-newsletters listing page ordered newest first.

  • newsletter_detail: Single newsletter showing its approved articles.

  • newsletter_create: Journalist or editor creates a new newsletter.

  • newsletter_edit: Journalist edits their own newsletter; editors may edit any newsletter on the platform.

  • newsletter_delete: Journalist or editor deletes a newsletter; confirmation page on GET, deletion on POST.

  • category_articles: Approved articles filtered by navigation category (uses Article.category, not newsletter category labels).

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.category_articles(request, slug)[source]

Show approved articles belonging to the selected navigation category.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.newsletter_create(request)[source]

Allow a journalist or editor to create a new newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.newsletter_delete(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own) or editor to delete a newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]

Render a single newsletter with its approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.newsletter_edit(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own newsletter) or editor to edit a newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletters.newsletter_list(request)[source]

Render the all-newsletters listing page.

daily_indaba.views.notifications module

Announcement / notification views for The Daily Indaba.

daily_indaba.views.notifications.announcement_detail(request, pk)[source]

Show an article-approval announcement to its recipient.

Marks the notification as seen on first view, then renders a role-aware page so the user can navigate to the article or their normal post-login destination.

If the user has more unseen notifications after this one, the template’s “Continue” button links to the next one so they are drained one at a time.

daily_indaba.views.notifications.dismiss_notification(request, pk)[source]

Mark a notification as seen without viewing the announcement page.

POST-only. Used by the dismiss button if a user wants to clear a notification from a list without navigating away.

daily_indaba.views.profiles module

Profile and publisher-management views for The Daily Indaba.

Internal helper: - _build_publisher_page_context: Builds the shared template context

for both the public profile and owner dashboard publisher views.

Publisher views: - publisher_profile: Public publisher profile showing approved

articles and subscription controls.

  • publisher_dashboard: Publisher account’s management dashboard (owner only); reuses the profile template with management controls.

  • publisher_manage_editors: Publisher assigns or removes affiliated editors (POST only; owner only).

Journalist views: - journalist_profile: Journalist’s public profile with their approved

articles and a subscribe toggle for readers.

  • journalist_list: Public alphabetical directory of all journalists.

Public directories: - publisher_list: Public alphabetical directory of all publishers.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.journalist_list(request)[source]

Public directory of all journalists on the platform.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.journalist_profile(request, pk)[source]

Display a journalist’s profile and their approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.publisher_dashboard(request)[source]

Display the signed-in publisher account’s management dashboard.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.publisher_list(request)[source]

Public directory of all publishers on the platform.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.publisher_manage_editors(request, pk)[source]

Allow a publisher account to assign or remove affiliated editors.

daily_indaba.views.profiles.publisher_profile(request, pk)[source]

Display a publisher’s public profile and its approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.static_pages module

Static / informational page views for The Daily Indaba.

  • about: Renders the About page.

  • contact: Renders the Contact page.

  • privacy_policy: Renders the Privacy Policy page.

daily_indaba.views.static_pages.about(request)[source]

About The Daily Indaba.

daily_indaba.views.static_pages.contact(request)[source]

Contact page.

daily_indaba.views.static_pages.privacy_policy(request)[source]

Privacy policy page.

Module contents

Views package for The Daily Indaba news app.

Importing from the sub-modules here means that urls.py continues to work without any change: it does from . import views and then accesses views.home, views.article_list, etc. exactly as before.

daily_indaba.views.about(request)[source]

About The Daily Indaba.

daily_indaba.views.announcement_detail(request, pk)[source]

Show an article-approval announcement to its recipient.

Marks the notification as seen on first view, then renders a role-aware page so the user can navigate to the article or their normal post-login destination.

If the user has more unseen notifications after this one, the template’s “Continue” button links to the next one so they are drained one at a time.

daily_indaba.views.approval_queue(request)[source]

Render the full article approval queue (editor only).

daily_indaba.views.article_approve(request, pk)[source]

Approve an article (editor only).

Sets approved=True and publication_date. Subscriber emails are dispatched by the article’s post-save signal after the approval is saved.

daily_indaba.views.article_create(request)[source]

Allow a journalist to create a new article.

daily_indaba.views.article_delete(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own unapproved article only) or editor to delete an article. Renders a confirmation page on GET; deletes on POST.

daily_indaba.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]

Render a single article with its threaded comment section.

Readers without a qualifying subscription see only the title, teaser (first sentence), and a subscribe prompt. Full access requires an active Subscription to the article’s author or publisher, or the all-articles flat-rate plan. Journalists and editors always have full access.

daily_indaba.views.article_edit(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist to edit their own unapproved article, or an editor to edit any article.

daily_indaba.views.article_list(request)[source]

Render the authenticated article listing page.

Supports filtering by importance level (GET param importance). The left sidebar displays importance-level links mirroring the eCommerce category panel.

daily_indaba.views.article_reject(request, pk)[source]

Reject (return) an article submission (editor only).

The article leaves the approval queue and is marked as returned so the journalist can revise and resubmit it. If a reason is supplied it is emailed to the journalist and stored on the article for in-app display.

daily_indaba.views.category_articles(request, slug)[source]

Show approved articles belonging to the selected navigation category.

daily_indaba.views.comment_confirm_delete(request, article_pk, comment_pk)[source]

Render a confirmation page before deleting a reader comment thread.

daily_indaba.views.comment_delete(request, article_pk, comment_pk)[source]

Allow a reader to delete their own comment thread.

daily_indaba.views.contact(request)[source]

Contact page.

daily_indaba.views.dismiss_notification(request, pk)[source]

Mark a notification as seen without viewing the announcement page.

POST-only. Used by the dismiss button if a user wants to clear a notification from a list without navigating away.

daily_indaba.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]

Render the editor overview dashboard with approval queue summary.

daily_indaba.views.home(request)[source]

Render the public home page.

Accessible to all visitors (no login required). Displays a one-sentence site description and the eight most recent approved articles, each shown with its title and the first sentence of its content only.

daily_indaba.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Render a journalist’s personal desk (articles + newsletters).

daily_indaba.views.journalist_list(request)[source]

Public directory of all journalists on the platform.

daily_indaba.views.journalist_profile(request, pk)[source]

Display a journalist’s profile and their approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.newsletter_create(request)[source]

Allow a journalist or editor to create a new newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletter_delete(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own) or editor to delete a newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]

Render a single newsletter with its approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.newsletter_edit(request, pk)[source]

Allow a journalist (own newsletter) or editor to edit a newsletter.

daily_indaba.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]

Render the all-newsletters listing page.

daily_indaba.views.post_comment(request, pk)[source]

Create a comment or reply on an article (readers only).

On validation failure the user is redirected back to the article with an error message rather than re-rendering the form, to keep the detail view simple.

daily_indaba.views.privacy_policy(request)[source]

Privacy policy page.

daily_indaba.views.publisher_dashboard(request)[source]

Display the signed-in publisher account’s management dashboard.

daily_indaba.views.publisher_list(request)[source]

Public directory of all publishers on the platform.

daily_indaba.views.publisher_manage_editors(request, pk)[source]

Allow a publisher account to assign or remove affiliated editors.

daily_indaba.views.publisher_profile(request, pk)[source]

Display a publisher’s public profile and its approved articles.

daily_indaba.views.publisher_set_fee(request, pk)[source]

Allow a publisher owner or affiliated editor to set the fee.

Valid range comes from the seeded pricing policy. Redirects back to the publisher’s profile page.

daily_indaba.views.set_journalist_fee(request)[source]

Allow a journalist to set their configured monthly subscription fee.

Validates the submitted fee and updates journalist_monthly_fee on the user. Redirects back to the journalist dashboard.

daily_indaba.views.subscribe_all_articles(request)[source]

Toggle the reader’s configured all-articles flat-rate plan.

Flips all_articles_plan and saves. When activated, a confirmation email is sent to the reader. Redirects to the subscriptions management page. Only readers may access this view.

daily_indaba.views.toggle_subscription(request)[source]

Toggle a reader’s subscription to a publisher or journalist.

Expects POST fields type (publisher | journalist) and id (primary key of the target). On a new subscription a confirmation email is sent to the reader. Redirects back to the referring page (or home if no referrer is set).
